original_text: Ethiopia Technology mix: 40% Smartphone adoption (2023), projected 50% by 2030
notes: Key ACCESS enabler: smartphone ownership is a prerequisite for app-based mobile money (Telebirr/M-Pesa) use, distinct from basic-phone USSD access. GSMA projects rise to 50% by 2030.

source_name: World Bank SDG7 / ESMAP Tracking SDG7 Electrification Database
original_text: Access to electricity (% of population) in Ethiopia was reported at 55.4% in 2023
notes: Enabler for agent-network uptime, POS/ATM operation, and smartphone charging. Large urban(94.7%)/rural(43.6%) gap plausibly underlies urban-rural account ownership gap; growth slowed to +0.39pp/yr 2020-23 vs +0.77pp/yr 2010-20, echoing the Access slowdown pattern.

indicator: Banking Business Proclamation No. 1360/2024 (Foreign Bank Entry)
original_text: On December 17, 2024, the Ethiopian Parliament approved the new Banking Business Proclamation No. 1360/2024, allowing foreign banks to re-enter the Ethiopian market after a 50-year absence
notes: First foreign bank entry law in 50 years; caps aggregate foreign ownership at 49%. Pillar intentionally left blank per unified-schema design principle; effects modeled via impact_link.

indicator: Foreign Bank Entry effect on Account Ownership
notes: Foreign bank entry expected to raise competition, product variety, and branch/agent investment, gradually lifting account ownership. Licensing and subsidiary set-up take 1-2 years, so effect is modeled as delayed/enabling rather than direct. Nigeria's phased bank liberalization used as a loose comparator; confidence is medium given no Ethiopia-specific pre/post data yet exists.

indicator: Foreign Bank Entry effect on Digital Payment Adoption
notes: Foreign banks often bring digital-first product design, per reporting on Ethiopia's 2025 banking deregulation (Wegagen/Abyssinia digital expansion). Kept low-confidence/negligible-magnitude given the long, uncertain path from proclamation to market presence.
